guardrails.title: Single Responsibility
guardrails.body: Each spec shall address one concern. Do not bundle unrelated changes into a single specification.

guardrails.title: Unambiguous Requirements
guardrails.body: Requirements shall avoid vague adjectives (e.g. "appropriate", "fast", "easy") and instead state verifiable conditions.

guardrails.title: Complete Context
guardrails.body: Each requirement shall pair a trigger condition (When / If) with an expected behavior (shall). Requirements without context or expected outcome are incomplete.

guardrails.title: No Hardcoded Secrets
guardrails.body: Source code shall not contain API keys, passwords, tokens, or other secrets directly. Externalize them via environment variables or configuration files.

guardrails.title: No Silent Error Swallowing
guardrails.body: Empty catch blocks or code that silently discards errors are prohibited. Errors shall be logged or re-thrown.

guardrails.title: Prioritize Requirements
guardrails.body: When requirements exceed three items, specify their priority order.

guardrails.title: Impact on Existing Features
guardrails.body: Changes shall list their impact on existing features. If no existing features are affected, state so explicitly.

guardrails.title: Bounded Resource Usage
guardrails.body: Recursive processing, retries, and bulk data loading shall have explicit upper bounds (depth, count, size). Designs that allow unbounded processing are prohibited.

guardrails.title: No Sensitive Data in Logs
guardrails.body: Logs shall not contain passwords, tokens, or personally identifiable information. Sensitive data shall be masked or excluded.

guardrails.title: No Disabling Existing Tests
guardrails.body: Skipping, commenting out, or deleting existing tests to make them pass is prohibited. When tests fail, fix the product code.

guardrails.title: Changes Require Test Coverage
guardrails.body: New features and bug fixes shall include tests covering at least the happy path. Changes without tests shall state the reason in the spec.

guardrails.title: Test Covers Spec Requirements
guardrails.body: Each spec requirement shall have a corresponding test that verifies it.

guardrails.title: Spec Includes Test Strategy
guardrails.body: The spec shall include a test strategy (what to test and how).

guardrails.title: Present Recommendations with Reasoning
guardrails.body: For each question, present a recommended answer. State the basis: (1) project docs/, (2) guardrail principles, or (3) existing code patterns. Lead with the recommendation and keep it concise.

guardrails.title: Explore Before Asking
guardrails.body: If a question can be answered by reading docs/ or exploring the codebase, do so before asking the user.

guardrails.title: Proactively Raise Related Implications
guardrails.body: When a decision has obvious related implications, raise them proactively. Do not wait for the user to notice the gap.

guardrails.title: Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing
guardrails.body: Before critiquing a decision, confirm whether the user is brainstorming or deciding. Do not evaluate brainstorming ideas as final decisions.

guardrails.title: Capture Digressions in draft.md
guardrails.body: When a discussion digresses, capture key insights and decisions in draft.md before returning to the main thread.

guardrails.title: Draft Stays at Requirements Level
guardrails.body: Draft is RFP/requirements level only. Do not include implementation details or function-level design.

guardrails.title: Synthesize Draft, Do Not Copy
guardrails.body: Organize and abstract draft content rather than copying it directly. Do not invent content not present in the draft.

guardrails.title: Record Unresolved Points as Open Questions
guardrails.body: Place unresolved points in the Open Questions section. Do not fabricate answers.

guardrails.title: Include 'Why This Approach' Rationale
guardrails.body: The spec shall include the rationale for why this approach was chosen.

guardrails.title: Escalate When New Tests Break Existing Tests
guardrails.body: If new tests cause existing tests to fail, inform the user and ask how to proceed.

guardrails.title: Do Not Modify Existing Tests Without Approval
guardrails.body: Do not modify or delete existing tests without user approval.

guardrails.title: Flag Tests That Become Irrelevant
guardrails.body: If past tests become irrelevant due to feature changes, flag them to the user.
